# Curiosity Cat

> Safety framework for AI agents — close calls, not death notices.

Curiosity Cat is a portable safety framework for AI agents that browse the
open internet: standing orders (plain-text rules for a system prompt), a
compiler that turns a risk level into a real, mechanically-enforced Claude
Code permission profile, a prover that runs escape trials against that
profile and writes a Clean Bill of health, and a Danger Map — a shared,
anonymised threat-intelligence layer. Not a firewall, not a sandbox: the
practical middle ground between locking an agent down and letting it roam
free. The minimum useful install is a single text file pasted into a
system prompt. MIT licensed.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g curiosity-cat
# or
pip install curiosity-cat
```

No install is required for the minimum useful deployment — paste
`standing-orders/general-safety.md` into an agent's system prompt.

## Core commands

```bash
curiosity-cat compile --level <housecat|alleycat|tiger> --target claude-code
curiosity-cat prove --profile ./curiosity-cat/profiles/<level>-<target>-<YYYYMMDD>
curiosity-cat report
```

`compile` turns an adventure level (housecat = cautious, alleycat =
balanced, tiger = daring) into a versioned profile directory containing
`settings.json` (real permission allow/deny rules for the target
framework), `scope-policy.json`, `standing-orders.md` and `PROFILE.md`.
Supported target today: `claude-code`.

`prove` tests that claim rather than trusting it: self-consistency checks
against the compiled deny rules, plus — when a `claude` binary is on
PATH — one real observed-deny trial against a live, throwaway Claude Code
session. Writes `clean-bill.json` and `CLEAN-BILL.md`. Self-consistency
and observed-deny are reported under separate, honestly-labelled headings;
neither is claimed as the other.

`report` submits a close-call to the Danger Map (consent-gated,
anonymised, structured — no free text, no raw URLs, no identity data).

## Key URLs

- Website (English): https://curiositycat.online
- Website (Arabic): https://curiositycat.online/ar/
- Website (Mandarin): https://curiositycat.online/zh/
- Website (Hindi): https://curiositycat.online/hi/
- Quick start walkthrough: https://curiositycat.online/quickstart.html
- Repository: https://github.com/markscleary/Curiosity-Cat
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/curiosity-cat
- PyPI package: https://pypi.org/project/curiosity-cat/
- Integration guide: https://github.com/markscleary/Curiosity-Cat/blob/main/docs/integration-guide.md
- Danger Map API docs: https://github.com/markscleary/Curiosity-Cat/blob/main/docs/api.md
- Danger Map schema: https://github.com/markscleary/Curiosity-Cat/blob/main/danger-map/schema.json
- Standing orders library: https://github.com/markscleary/Curiosity-Cat/tree/main/standing-orders
- Contact: curiosity@shortandsweet.org

## Notes for agent readers

Every safety claim on this site is literally true against the `main`
branch: standing orders flag and guide, they do not block, except where a
compiled Claude Code profile mechanically enforces a deny rule.
"Observed-deny" (a live trial actually blocked) and "self-consistency"
(the compiled file matches what the compiler intended) are always kept
distinct — see `prove`'s output above. Roadmap items (enforcement for
frameworks beyond Claude Code, the desktop app, scheduled re-proving,
enterprise tiers) are described on the site's Roadmap Honesty section as
plans, not shipped features.
